description.content: Public market data endpoints for order book depth, recent trades, aggregate trades, klines, ticker prices, and average prices.
Get the current order book depth for a given trading pair. Returns bids and asks with price and quantity at each level.

query.description: (Required) Trading pair symbol, e.g. BTCUSDT.
Number of price levels to return. Default 100. Valid limits are 5, 10, 20, 50, 100, 500, 1000, 5000.

Start time in milliseconds (inclusive).
End time in milliseconds (inclusive).
Number of results to return. Default 500, max 1000.

Response type. FULL or MINI.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Trading endpoints for placing, querying, and canceling orders on the spot exchange. Requires SIGNED authentication.
Send in a new order. Requires SIGNED authentication with the TRADE permission. Order types include LIMIT, MARKET, STOP_LOSS, STOP_LOSS_LIMIT, TAKE_PROFIT, TAKE_PROFIT_LIMIT, and LIMIT_MAKER.

query.description: (Required) Trading pair symbol, e.g. BTCUSDT.
(Required) Order side.
(Required) Order type.
Time in force policy for the order.
Order quantity in base asset.
Order quantity in quote asset. Used for MARKET orders.
Order price. Required for LIMIT orders.
A unique ID for the order. Automatically generated if not sent.
Arbitrary numeric value identifying the strategy.
Arbitrary numeric value identifying the strategy type. Values smaller than 1000000 are reserved.
Used with STOP_LOSS, STOP_LOSS_LIMIT, TAKE_PROFIT, and TAKE_PROFIT_LIMIT orders.
Used with STOP_LOSS, STOP_LOSS_LIMIT, TAKE_PROFIT, and TAKE_PROFIT_LIMIT orders. Basis points.
Used with LIMIT, STOP_LOSS_LIMIT, and TAKE_PROFIT_LIMIT to create an iceberg order.
Set the response JSON. ACK, RESULT, or FULL.
The self-trade prevention mode.
The number of milliseconds after timestamp the request is valid for. Default 5000, max 60000.
(Required) Request timestamp in milliseconds since Unix epoch.
(Required) HMAC SHA256 signature of the total query string using the API secret key.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Endpoints for managing user data stream listen keys used to subscribe to account update WebSocket streams.

Start a new user data stream. The stream is closed after 60 minutes unless a keepalive is sent. If the account has an active listenKey, that listenKey will be returned and its validity extended for 60 minutes.
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Keepalive a user data stream to prevent a timeout. User data streams will close after 60 minutes. It is recommended to send a ping about every 30 minutes.

query.description: (Required) The listen key to keep alive.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Close out a user data stream.

query.description: (Required) The listen key to close.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

The Binance Spot Trading REST API provides programmatic access to the Binance spot exchange, the world's largest cryptocurrency trading platform by volume. Developers can place and manage orders, query account balances, retrieve real-time and historical market data, and manage trading pairs. The API supports limit, market, stop-loss, and other order types, along with account and trade history endpoints. Authentication uses HMAC SHA256 signed requests with API key and secret key credentials.

Contact Support:
 Name: Binance Support
